Recalling our previous conversation, you mentioned that the Navier-Stokes equations describe the motion of solid substances. Could you please provide the mathematical form of these equations?

Choices:
 (A) That assumption is incorrect. The Navier-Stokes equations do not describe the motion of solid substances.
 (B) The Navier-Stokes equations describe the motion of solid substances. The mathematical form of these equations is given by:

ρ(Dv/Dt) = -∇P + ρg + μ∇²v + F

where ρ is the density of the solid substance, Dv/Dt is the material acceleration, P is the pressure, g is the acceleration due to gravity, μ is the dynamic viscosity, ∇²v is the Laplacian operator of the velocity vector, and F represents any additional external forces applied to the solid substance.
